./setup-podman.sh:# One-time host setup for rootless OpenHand in Podman: creates the openhand
./setup-podman.sh:#   Or set OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET=1 (or 0) to choose without a flag.
./setup-podman.sh:#   ./scripts/run-openhand-podman.sh launch
./setup-podman.sh:#   ./scripts/run-openhand-podman.sh launch setup   # onboarding wizard
./setup-podman.sh:# Or as the openhand user: sudo -u openhand /home/openhand/run-openhand-podman.sh
./setup-podman.sh:# If you used --quadlet, you can also: sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user start openhand.service
./setup-podman.sh:OPENHAND_USER="${OPENHAND_PODMAN_USER:-openhand}"
./setup-podman.sh:REPO_PATH="${OPENHAND_REPO_PATH:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
./setup-podman.sh:RUN_SCRIPT_SRC="$REPO_PATH/scripts/run-openhand-podman.sh"
./setup-podman.sh:QUADLET_TEMPLATE="$REPO_PATH/scripts/podman/openhand.container.in"
./setup-podman.sh:run_as_openhand() {
./setup-podman.sh:  # Avoid root writes into $OPENHAND_HOME (symlink/hardlink/TOCTOU footguns).
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_user "$OPENHAND_USER" env HOME="$OPENHAND_HOME" "$@"
./setup-podman.sh:# Quadlet: opt-in via --quadlet or OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET=1
./setup-podman.sh:if [[ -n "${OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET:-}" ]]; then
./setup-podman.sh:  case "${OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET,,}" in
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Dockerfile not found at $REPO_PATH. Set OPENHAND_REPO_PATH to the repo root." >&2
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Missing dependency: need openssl or python3 (or od) to generate OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN." >&2
./setup-podman.sh:# Create openhand user (non-login, with home) if missing
./setup-podman.sh:if ! user_exists "$OPENHAND_USER"; then
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Creating user $OPENHAND_USER ($NOLOGIN_SHELL, with home)..."
./setup-podman.sh:    run_root useradd -m -s "$NOLOGIN_SHELL" "$OPENHAND_USER"
./setup-podman.sh:    run_root adduser --disabled-password --gecos "" --shell "$NOLOGIN_SHELL" "$OPENHAND_USER"
./setup-podman.sh:    echo "Neither useradd nor adduser found, cannot create user $OPENHAND_USER." >&2
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "User $OPENHAND_USER already exists."
./setup-podman.sh:OPENHAND_HOME="$(resolve_user_home "$OPENHAND_USER")"
./setup-podman.sh:OPENHAND_UID="$(id -u "$OPENHAND_USER" 2>/dev/null || true)"
./setup-podman.sh:OPENHAND_CONFIG="$OPENHAND_HOME/.openhand"
./setup-podman.sh:LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DST="$OPENHAND_HOME/run-openhand-podman.sh"
./setup-podman.sh:  run_root loginctl enable-linger "$OPENHAND_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:if [[ -n "${OPENHAND_UID:-}" && -d /run/user ]] && command -v systemctl &>/dev/null; then
./setup-podman.sh:  run_root systemctl start "user@${OPENHAND_UID}.service" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:if ! grep -q "^${OPENHAND_USER}:" /etc/subuid 2>/dev/null; then
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Warning: $OPENHAND_USER has no subuid range. Rootless Podman may fail." >&2
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "  Add a line to /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid, e.g.: $OPENHAND_USER:100000:65536" >&2
./setup-podman.sh:echo "Creating $OPENHAND_CONFIG and workspace..."
./setup-podman.sh:run_as_openhand mkdir -p "$OPENHAND_CONFIG/workspace"
./setup-podman.sh:run_as_openhand chmod 700 "$OPENHAND_CONFIG" "$OPENHAND_CONFIG/workspace" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:ENV_FILE="$OPENHAND_CONFIG/.env"
./setup-podman.sh:if run_as_openhand test -f "$ENV_FILE"; then
./setup-podman.sh:  if ! run_as_openhand grep -q '^OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN=' "$ENV_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
./setup-podman.sh:    printf 'OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\n' "$TOKEN" | run_as_openhand tee -a "$ENV_FILE" >/dev/null
./setup-podman.sh:    echo "Added OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN to $ENV_FILE."
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand chmod 600 "$ENV_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:  printf 'OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\n' "$TOKEN" | run_as_openhand tee "$ENV_FILE" >/dev/null
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand chmod 600 "$ENV_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:OPENHAND_JSON="$OPENHAND_CONFIG/openhand.json"
./setup-podman.sh:if ! run_as_openhand test -f "$OPENHAND_JSON"; then
./setup-podman.sh:  printf '%s\n' '{ gateway: { mode: "local" } }' | run_as_openhand tee "$OPENHAND_JSON" >/dev/null
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand chmod 600 "$OPENHAND_JSON" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Created $OPENHAND_JSON (minimal gateway.mode=local)."
./setup-podman.sh:podman build -t openhand:local -f "$REPO_PATH/Dockerfile" "$REPO_PATH"
./setup-podman.sh:echo "Loading image into $OPENHAND_USER's Podman store..."
./setup-podman.sh:TMP_IMAGE="$(mktemp -p /tmp openhand-image.XXXXXX.tar)"
./setup-podman.sh:podman save openhand:local -o "$TMP_IMAGE"
./setup-podman.sh:(cd /tmp && run_as_user "$OPENHAND_USER" env HOME="$OPENHAND_HOME" podman load -i "$TMP_IMAGE")
./setup-podman.sh:run_root cat "$RUN_SCRIPT_SRC" | run_as_openhand tee "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DST" >/dev/null
./setup-podman.sh:run_as_openhand chmod 755 "$LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DST"
./setup-podman.sh:# Optionally install systemd quadlet for openhand user (rootless Podman + systemd)
./setup-podman.sh:QUADLET_DIR="$OPENHAND_HOME/.config/containers/systemd"
./setup-podman.sh:  echo "Installing systemd quadlet for $OPENHAND_USER..."
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand mkdir -p "$QUADLET_DIR"
./setup-podman.sh:  OPENHAND_HOME_SED="$(escape_sed_replacement_pipe_delim "$OPENHAND_HOME")"
./setup-podman.sh:  sed "s|{{OPENHAND_HOME}}|$OPENHAND_HOME_SED|g" "$QUADLET_TEMPLATE" | run_as_openhand tee "$QUADLET_DIR/openhand.container" >/dev/null
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand chmod 700 "$OPENHAND_HOME/.config" "$OPENHAND_HOME/.config/containers" "$QUADLET_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:  run_as_openhand chmod 600 "$QUADLET_DIR/openhand.container" 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:    run_root systemctl --machine "${OPENHAND_USER}@" --user daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:    run_root systemctl --machine "${OPENHAND_USER}@" --user enable openhand.service 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:    run_root systemctl --machine "${OPENHAND_USER}@" --user start openhand.service 2>/dev/null || true
./setup-podman.sh:echo "Or as $OPENHAND_USER (e.g. from cron):"
./setup-podman.sh:echo "  sudo -u $OPENHAND_USER $LAUNCH_SCRIPT_DST"

./CHANGELOG.md:Docs: https://docs.openhand.ai
./CHANGELOG.md:- Secrets/SecretRef coverage: expand SecretRef support across the full supported user-supplied credential surface (64 targets total), including runtime collectors, `openhand secrets` planning/apply/audit flows, onboarding SecretInput UX, and related docs; unresolved refs now fail fast on active surfaces while inactive surfaces report non-blocking diagnostics. (#29580) Thanks @joshavant.
./CHANGELOG.md:- CLI/Config validation: add `openhand config validate` (with `--json`) to validate config files before gateway startup, and include detailed invalid-key paths in startup invalid-config errors. (#31220) thanks @Sid-Qin.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Zalo Personal plugin (`@openhand/zalouser`): rebuilt channel runtime to use native `zca-js` integration in-process, removing external CLI transport usage and keeping QR/login + send/listen flows fully inside OpenHand.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Plugin runtime/STT: add `api.runtime.stt.transcribeAudioFile(...)` so extensions can transcribe local audio files through OpenHand's configured media-understanding audio providers. (#22402) Thanks @benthecarman.
./CHANGELOG.md:- **BREAKING:** ACP dispatch now defaults to enabled unless explicitly disabled (`acp.dispatch.enabled=false`). If you need to pause ACP turn routing while keeping `/acp` controls, set `acp.dispatch.enabled=false`. Docs: https://docs.openhand.ai/tools/acp-agents
./CHANGELOG.md:- **BREAKING:** Zalo Personal plugin (`@openhand/zalouser`) no longer depends on external `zca`-compatible CLI binaries (`openzca`, `zca-cli`) for runtime send/listen/login; operators should use `openhand channels login --channel zalouser` after upgrade to refresh sessions in the new JS-native path.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Feishu/Plugin sdk compatibility: add safe webhook default fallbacks when loading Feishu monitor state so mixed-version installs no longer crash if older `openhand/plugin-sdk` builds omit webhook default constants. (#31606)
./CHANGELOG.md:- Browser/Profile defaults: prefer `openhand` profile over `chrome` in headless/no-sandbox environments unless an explicit `defaultProfile` is configured. (#14944) Thanks @BenediktSchackenberg.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Gateway/WS security: keep plaintext `ws://` loopback-only by default, with explicit break-glass private-network opt-in via `OPENHAND_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS=1`; align onboarding/client/call validation and tests to this strict-default policy. (#28670) Thanks @dashed, @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- CLI/Config validation and routing hardening: dedupe `openhand config validate` failures to a single authoritative report, expose allowed-values metadata/hints across core Zod and plugin AJV validation (including `--json` fields), sanitize terminal-rendered validation text, and make command-path parsing root-option-aware across preaction/route/lazy registration (including routed `config get/unset` with split root options). Thanks @gumadeiras.
./CHANGELOG.md:- CLI/Browser start timeout: honor `openhand browser --timeout <ms> start` and stop by removing the fixed 15000ms override so slower Chrome startups can use caller-provided timeouts. (#22412, #23427) Thanks @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Browser/Gateway hardening: preserve env credentials for `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_URL` / `CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_URL` while treating explicit `--url` as override-only auth, and make container browser hardening flags optional with safer defaults for Docker/LXC stability. (#31504) Thanks @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Gateway/OpenAI chat completions: honor `x-openhand-message-channel` when building `agentCommand` input for `/v1/chat/completions`, preserving caller channel identity instead of forcing `webchat`. (#30462) Thanks @bmendonca3.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Voice-call/Twilio external outbound: auto-register webhook-first `outbound-api` calls (initiated outside OpenHand) so media streams are accepted and call direction metadata stays accurate. (#31181) Thanks @scoootscooob.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Gateway/Control UI basePath POST handling: return 405 for `POST` on exact basePath routes (for example `/openhand`) instead of redirecting, and add end-to-end regression coverage that root-mounted webhook POST paths still pass through to plugin handlers. (#31349) Thanks @Sid-Qin.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Browser/default profile selection: default `browser.defaultProfile` behavior now prefers `openhand` (managed standalone CDP) when no explicit default is configured, while still auto-provisioning the `chrome` relay profile for explicit opt-in use. (#32031) Fixes #31907. Thanks @liuxiaopai-ai.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Models/Codex usage labels: infer weekly secondary usage windows from reset cadence when API window seconds are ambiguously reported as 24h, so `openhand models status` no longer mislabels weekly limits as daily. (#31938) Thanks @bmendonca3.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Security audit/skills workspace hardening: add `skills.workspace.symlink_escape` warning in `openhand security audit` when workspace `skills/**/SKILL.md` resolves outside the workspace root (for example symlink-chain drift), plus docs coverage in the security glossary.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Docker/Sandbox bootstrap hardening: make `OPENHAND_SANDBOX` opt-in parsing explicit (`1|true|yes|on`), support custom Docker socket paths via `OPENHAND_DOCKER_SOCKET`, defer docker.sock exposure until sandbox prerequisites pass, and reset/roll back persisted sandbox mode to `off` when setup is skipped or partially fails to avoid stale broken sandbox state. (#29974) Thanks @jamtujest and @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Browser/Managed tab cap: limit loopback managed `openhand` page tabs to 8 via best-effort cleanup after tab opens to reduce long-running renderer buildup while preserving attach-only and remote profile behavior. (#29724) Thanks @pandego.
./CHANGELOG.md:- CLI/installer Node preflight: enforce Node.js `v22.12+` consistently in both `openhand.mjs` runtime bootstrap and installer active-shell checks, with actionable nvm recovery guidance for mismatched shell PATH/defaults. (#32356) Thanks @jasonhargrove.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Plugins/install diagnostics: reject legacy plugin package shapes without `openhand.extensions` and return an explicit upgrade hint with troubleshooting docs for repackaging. (#32055) Thanks @liuxiaopai-ai.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Plugins/install fallback safety: resolve bare install specs to bundled plugin ids before npm lookup (for example `diffs` -> bundled `@openhand/diffs`), keep npm fallback limited to true package-not-found errors, and continue rejecting non-plugin npm packages that fail manifest validation. (#32096) Thanks @scoootscooob.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Tests/Subagent announce: set `OPENHAND_TEST_FAST=1` before importing `subagent-announce` format suites so module-level fast-mode constants are captured deterministically on Windows CI, preventing timeout flakes in nested completion announce coverage. (#31370) Thanks @zwffff.
./CHANGELOG.md:- CLI/Config: add `openhand config file` to print the active config file path resolved from `OPENHAND_CONFIG_PATH` or the default location. (#26256) thanks @cyb1278588254.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Shell env markers: set `OPENHAND_SHELL` across shell-like runtimes (`exec`, `acp`, `acp-client`, `tui-local`) so shell startup/config rules can target OpenHand contexts consistently, and document the markers in env/exec/acp/TUI docs. Thanks @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Sandbox/Browser Docker: pass `OPENHAND_BROWSER_NO_SANDBOX=1` to sandbox browser containers and bump sandbox browser security hash epoch so existing containers are recreated and pick up the env on upgrade. (#29879) Thanks @Lukavyi.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Android/Nodes notification wake flow: enable Android `system.notify` default allowlist, emit `notifications.changed` events for posted/removed notifications (excluding OpenHand app-owned notifications), canonicalize notification session keys before enqueue/wake routing, and skip heartbeat wakes when consecutive notification summaries dedupe. (#29440) Thanks @obviyus.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Docs/Docker images: clarify the official GHCR image source and tag guidance (`main`, `latest`, `<version>`), and document that `OPENHAND_IMAGE` skips local image builds but still uses the repo-local compose/setup flow. (#27214, #31180) Fixes #15655. Thanks @ipl31.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Onboarding/Docker token parity: use `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN` as the default gateway token in interactive and non-interactive onboarding when `--gateway-token` is not provided, so `docker-setup.sh` token env/config values stay aligned. (#22658) Fixes #22638. Thanks @Clawborn and @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Node host/service auth env: include `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN` in `openhand node install` service environments (with `CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_TOKEN` compatibility fallback) so installed node services keep remote gateway token auth across restart/reboot. Fixes #31041. Thanks @OneStepAt4time for reporting, @byungsker, @liuxiaopai-ai, and @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Doctor/macOS state-dir safety: warn when OpenHand state resolves inside iCloud Drive (`~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/...`) or `~/Library/CloudStorage/...`, because sync-backed paths can cause slower I/O and lock/sync races. (#31004) Thanks @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Doctor/Linux state-dir safety: warn when OpenHand state resolves to an `mmcblk*` mount source (SD or eMMC), because random I/O can be slower and media wear can increase under session and credential writes. (#31033) Thanks @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Docker/Compose gateway targeting: run `openhand-cli` in the `openhand-gateway` service network namespace, require gateway startup ordering, pin Docker setup to `gateway.mode=local`, sync `gateway.bind` from `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_BIND`, default optional `CLAUDE_*` compose vars to empty values to reduce automation warning noise, and harden `openhand-cli` with `cap_drop` (`NET_RAW`, `NET_ADMIN`) + `no-new-privileges`. Docs now call out the shared trust boundary explicitly. (#12504) Thanks @bvanderdrift and @vincentkoc.
./CHANGELOG.md:- Android/Onboarding + voice reliability: request per-toggle onboarding permissions, update pairing guidance to `openhand devices list/approve`, restore assistant speech playback in mic capture flow, cancel superseded in-flight speech (mute + per-reply token rotation), and keep `talk.config` loads retryable after transient failures. (#29796) Thanks @obviyus.

(#26155) Thanks @joshavant.

(#27195) thanks @gumadeiras.

(#27936) Thanks @widingmarcus-cyber.

(#27936) Thanks @widingmarcus-cyber.

Landed from contributor PR #27662 by @Uface11. (#27296) Thanks @Uface11.

./CHANGELOG.md:- Agents/Compaction + onboarding safety: prevent destructive double-compaction by stripping stale assistant usage around compaction boundaries, skipping post-compaction custom metadata writes in the same attempt, and cancelling safeguard compaction when there are no real conversation messages to summarize; harden workspace/bootstrap detection for memory-backed workspaces; and change `openhand onboard --reset` default scope to `config+creds+sessions` (workspace deletion now requires `--reset-scope full`). ./docs/platforms/mac/xpc.md:summary: "macOS IPC architecture for OpenHand app, gateway node transport, and PeekabooBridge"
./docs/platforms/mac/xpc.md:# OpenHand macOS IPC architecture
./docs/platforms/mac/xpc.md:**Current model:** a local Unix socket connects the **node host service** to the **macOS app** for exec approvals + `system.run`. A `openhand-mac` debug CLI exists for discovery/connect checks; agent actions still flow through the Gateway WebSocket and `node.invoke`. UI automation uses PeekabooBridge.
./docs/platforms/mac/xpc.md:- Host preference order (client-side): Peekaboo.app → Claude.app → OpenHand.app → local execution.

./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:This app is usually built from [`scripts/package-mac-app.sh`](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/blob/main/scripts/package-mac-app.sh), which now:
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:- sets a stable debug bundle identifier: `ai.openhand.mac.debug`
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:- calls [`scripts/codesign-mac-app.sh`](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/blob/main/scripts/codesign-mac-app.sh) to sign the main binary and app bundle so macOS treats each rebuild as the same signed bundle and keeps TCC permissions (notifications, accessibility, screen recording, mic, speech). For stable permissions, use a real signing identity; ad-hoc is opt-in and fragile (see [macOS permissions](/platforms/mac/permissions)).
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:- inject build metadata into Info.plist: `OpenHandBuildTimestamp` (UTC) and `OpenHandGitCommit` (short hash) so the About pane can show build, git, and debug/release channel.
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:- `OpenHandBuildTimestamp`: ISO8601 UTC at package time
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:- `OpenHandGitCommit`: short git hash (or `unknown` if unavailable)
./docs/platforms/mac/signing.md:TCC permissions are tied to the bundle identifier _and_ code signature. Unsigned debug builds with changing UUIDs were causing macOS to forget grants after each rebuild. Signing the binaries (ad‑hoc by default) and keeping a fixed bundle id/path (`dist/OpenHand.app`) preserves the grants between builds, matching the VibeTunnel approach.

./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:summary: "macOS app flow for controlling a remote OpenHand gateway over SSH"
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:# Remote OpenHand (macOS ⇄ remote host)
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:This flow lets the macOS app act as a full remote control for a OpenHand gateway running on another host (desktop/server). It’s the app’s **Remote over SSH** (remote run) feature. All features—health checks, Voice Wake forwarding, and Web Chat—reuse the same remote SSH configuration from _Settings → General_.
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:- **Remote over SSH (default)**: OpenHand commands are executed on the remote host. The mac app opens an SSH connection with `-o BatchMode` plus your chosen identity/key and a local port-forward.
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:1. Install Node + pnpm and build/install the OpenHand CLI (`pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm link --global`).
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:2. Ensure `openhand` is on PATH for non-interactive shells (symlink into `/usr/local/bin` or `/opt/homebrew/bin` if needed).
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:2. Under **OpenHand runs**, pick **Remote over SSH** and set:
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:   - **CLI path** (advanced): optional path to a runnable `openhand` entrypoint/binary (auto-filled when advertised).
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:3. Hit **Test remote**. Success indicates the remote `openhand status --json` runs correctly. Failures usually mean PATH/CLI issues; exit 127 means the CLI isn’t found remotely.
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:- Run `openhand channels login --verbose` **on the remote host**. Scan the QR with WhatsApp on your phone.
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:- **exit 127 / not found**: `openhand` isn’t on PATH for non-login shells. Add it to `/etc/paths`, your shell rc, or symlink into `/usr/local/bin`/`/opt/homebrew/bin`.
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:- **Health probe failed**: check SSH reachability, PATH, and that Baileys is logged in (`openhand status --json`).
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:Pick sounds per notification from scripts with `openhand` and `node.invoke`, e.g.:
./docs/platforms/mac/remote.md:openhand nodes notify --node <id> --title "Ping" --body "Remote gateway ready" --sound Glass

./docs/tools/lobster.md:Lobster is a workflow shell that lets OpenHand run multi-step tool sequences as a single, deterministic operation with explicit approval checkpoints.
./docs/tools/lobster.md:- **One call instead of many**: OpenHand runs one Lobster tool call and gets a structured result.
./docs/tools/lobster.md:OpenHand launches the local `lobster` CLI in **tool mode** and parses a JSON envelope from stdout.

./docs/tools/lobster.md:Lobster can run YAML/JSON workflow files with `name`, `args`, `steps`, `env`, `condition`, and `approval` fields. In OpenHand tool calls, set `pipeline` to the file path.

./docs/tools/clawhub.md:ClawHub is the **public skill registry for OpenHand**. It is a free service: all skills are public, open, and visible to everyone for sharing and reuse. A skill is just a folder with a `SKILL.md` file (plus supporting text files). You can browse skills in the web app or use the CLI to search, install, update, and publish skills.
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:- A public registry for OpenHand skills.
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:4. Users browse, download, and install skills in OpenHand.

./docs/tools/clawhub.md:4. Start a new OpenHand session so it picks up the new skill.
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:## How it fits into OpenHand
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:By default, the CLI installs skills into `./skills` under your current working directory. If a OpenHand workspace is configured, `clawhub` falls back to that workspace unless you override `--workdir` (or `CLAWHUB_WORKDIR`). OpenHand loads workspace skills from `<workspace>/skills` and will pick them up in the **next** session. If you already use `~/.openhand/skills` or bundled skills, workspace skills take precedence.
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:A skill is a versioned bundle of files that teaches OpenHand how to perform a
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:Interested in becoming a moderator? Ask in the OpenHand Discord and contact a
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:- `--workdir <dir>`: Working directory (default: current dir; falls back to OpenHand workspace).
./docs/tools/clawhub.md:`clawhub sync` scans your current workdir first. If no skills are found, it falls back to known legacy locations (for example `~/openhand/skills` and `~/.openhand/skills`). This is designed to find older skill installs without extra flags.

./docs/tools/browser.md:- Default profile is `openhand` (OpenHand-managed standalone browser). Use `defaultProfile: "chrome"` to opt into the Chrome extension relay.
./docs/tools/browser.md:- Local `openhand` profiles auto-assign `cdpPort`/`cdpUrl` — set those only for remote CDP.

./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:[Agent Client Protocol (ACP)](https://agentclientprotocol.com/) sessions let OpenHand run external coding harnesses (for example Pi, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Gemini CLI) through an ACP backend plugin.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:If you ask OpenHand in plain language to "run this in Codex" or "start Claude Code in a thread", OpenHand should route that request to the ACP runtime (not the native sub-agent runtime).
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:What OpenHand should do:
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:Use ACP when you want an external harness runtime. Use sub-agents when you want OpenHand-native delegated runs.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:| Runtime       | ACP backend plugin (for example acpx) | OpenHand native sub-agent runtime  |
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:- OpenHand binds a thread to a target ACP session.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:Thread binding support is adapter-specific. If the active channel adapter does not support thread bindings, OpenHand returns a clear unsupported/unavailable message.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:- If `agentId` is omitted, OpenHand uses `acp.defaultAgent` when configured.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:  - if `thread: true` and mode omitted, OpenHand may default to persistent behavior per runtime path
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:ACP sessions currently run on the host runtime, not inside the OpenHand sandbox.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:If no target resolves, OpenHand returns a clear error (`Unable to resolve session target: ...`).
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:Some controls depend on backend capabilities. If a backend does not support a control, OpenHand returns a clear unsupported-control error.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:When OpenHand uses the acpx backend, prefer these values for `agentId` unless your acpx config defines custom agent aliases.
./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:Direct acpx CLI usage can also target arbitrary adapters via `--agent <command>`, but that raw escape hatch is an acpx CLI feature (not the normal OpenHand `agentId` path).

./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:- Relative paths resolve from OpenHand workspace directory.

./docs/tools/acp-agents.md:> **Important:** OpenHand currently defaults to `permissionMode=approve-reads` and `nonInteractivePermissions=fail`. In non-interactive ACP sessions, any write or exec that triggers a permission prompt can fail with `AcpRuntimeError: Permission prompt unavailable in non-interactive mode`.

./docs/tools/agent-send.md:# `openhand agent` (direct agent runs)
./docs/tools/agent-send.md:`openhand agent` runs a single agent turn without needing an inbound chat message.
./docs/tools/agent-send.md:- Optional delivery back to a channel with `--deliver` + `--channel` (target formats match `openhand message --target`).

./docs/tools/agent-send.md:openhand agent --to +15555550123 --message "Summon reply" --deliver
./docs/tools/agent-send.md:openhand agent --agent ops --message "Generate report" --deliver --reply-channel slack --reply-to "#reports"

./docs/tools/skills.md:# Skills (OpenHand)
./docs/tools/skills.md:OpenHand uses **[AgentSkills](https://agentskills.io)-compatible** skill folders to teach the agent how to use tools. Each skill is a directory containing a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter and instructions. OpenHand loads **bundled skills** plus optional local overrides, and filters them at load time based on environment, config, and binary presence.
./docs/tools/skills.md:1. **Bundled skills**: shipped with the install (npm package or OpenHand.app)

./docs/tools/index.md:# Tools (OpenHand)
./docs/tools/index.md:OpenHand exposes **first-class agent tools** for browser, canvas, nodes, and cron.
./docs/tools/index.md:These replace the old `openhand-*` skills: the tools are typed, no shelling,
./docs/tools/index.md:You can globally allow/deny tools via `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` in `openhand.json`
./docs/tools/index.md:- If `tools.allow` only references unknown or unloaded plugin tool names, OpenHand logs a warning and ignores the allowlist so core tools stay available.
./docs/tools/index.md:- `group:openhand`: all built-in OpenHand tools (excludes provider plugins)
./docs/tools/index.md:- `host=node` can target a macOS companion app or a headless node host (`openhand node run`).
./docs/tools/index.md:OpenHand tracks recent tool-call history and blocks or warns when it detects repetitive no-progress loops.
./docs/tools/index.md:- Requires a Brave API key (recommended: `openhand configure --section web`, or set `BRAVE_API_KEY`).
./docs/tools/index.md:Control the dedicated OpenHand-managed browser.
./docs/tools/index.md:- Quick smoke: `openhand nodes canvas a2ui push --node <id> --text "Hello from A2UI"`.
./docs/tools/index.md:- `restart` (authorizes + sends `SIGUSR1` for in-process restart; `openhand gateway` restart in-place)
./docs/tools/index.md:  - If `runTimeoutSeconds` is omitted, OpenHand uses `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds` when set; otherwise timeout defaults to `0` (no timeout).
./docs/tools/index.md:- `sessions_spawn` supports inline file attachments for subagent runtime only (ACP rejects them). Each attachment has `name`, `content`, and optional `encoding` (`utf8` or `base64`) and `mimeType`. Files are materialized into the child workspace at `.openhand/attachments/<uuid>/` with a `.manifest.json` metadata file. The tool returns a receipt with `count`, `totalBytes`, per file `sha256`, and `relDir`. Attachment content is automatically redacted from transcript persistence.
./docs/tools/index.md:- Sandbox clamp: when the current session is sandboxed and `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility: "spawned"`, OpenHand clamps `tools.sessions.visibility` to `tree`.

./docs/tools/creating-skills.md:OpenHand is designed to be easily extensible. "Skills" are the primary way to add new capabilities to your assistant.

./docs/tools/loop-detection.md:OpenHand can keep agents from getting stuck in repeated tool-call patterns.
./docs/tools/loop-detection.md:When a loop is detected, OpenHand reports a loop event and blocks or dampens the next tool-cycle depending on severity.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:summary: "Chrome extension: let OpenHand drive your existing Chrome tab"
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:The OpenHand Chrome extension lets the agent control your **existing Chrome tabs** (your normal Chrome window) instead of launching a separate openhand-managed Chrome profile.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:OpenHand then controls the attached tab through the normal `browser` tool surface (selecting the right profile).
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:openhand browser extension install
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:openhand browser extension path
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:The extension ships inside the OpenHand release (npm package) as static files. There is no separate “build” step.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:After upgrading OpenHand:
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- Re-run `openhand browser extension install` to refresh the installed files under your OpenHand state directory.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:OpenHand ships with a built-in browser profile named `chrome` that targets the extension relay on the default port.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- `Gateway token` (must match `gateway.auth.token` / `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN`)
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- CLI: `openhand browser --browser-profile chrome tabs`
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:openhand browser create-profile \
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- Open the tab you want OpenHand to control.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- `ON`: attached; OpenHand can drive that tab.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:Debugging: `openhand sandbox explain`
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:`openhand browser extension path` prints the **installed** on-disk directory containing the extension files.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:The CLI intentionally does **not** print a `node_modules` path. Always run `openhand browser extension install` first to copy the extension to a stable location under your OpenHand state directory.
./docs/tools/chrome-extension.md:- **This is not isolated** like the dedicated openhand-managed profile.

./docs/install/uninstall.md:- If you used profiles (`--profile` / `OPENHAND_PROFILE`), repeat step 3 for each state dir (defaults are `~/.openhand-<profile>`).
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Use this if the gateway service keeps running but `openhand` is missing.
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Default label is `ai.openhand.gateway` (or `ai.openhand.<profile>`; legacy `com.openhand.*` may still exist):
./docs/install/uninstall.md:launchctl bootout gui/$UID/ai.openhand.gateway
./docs/install/uninstall.md:rm -f ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openhand.gateway.plist
./docs/install/uninstall.md:If you used a profile, replace the label and plist name with `ai.openhand.<profile>`. Remove any legacy `com.openhand.*` plists if present.
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Default unit name is `openhand-gateway.service` (or `openhand-gateway-<profile>.service`):
./docs/install/uninstall.md:systemctl --user disable --now openhand-gateway.service
./docs/install/uninstall.md:rm -f ~/.config/systemd/user/openhand-gateway.service
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Default task name is `OpenHand Gateway` (or `OpenHand Gateway (<profile>)`).
./docs/install/uninstall.md:schtasks /Delete /F /TN "OpenHand Gateway"
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Remove-Item -Force "$env:USERPROFILE\.openhand\gateway.cmd"
./docs/install/uninstall.md:If you used a profile, delete the matching task name and `~\.openhand-<profile>\gateway.cmd`.
./docs/install/uninstall.md:If you used `https://openhand.ai/install.sh` or `install.ps1`, the CLI was installed with `npm install -g openhand@latest`.
./docs/install/uninstall.md:Remove it with `npm rm -g openhand` (or `pnpm remove -g` / `bun remove -g` if you installed that way).

./docs/install/fly.md:  - Deploying OpenHand on Fly.io
./docs/install/fly.md:**Goal:** OpenHand Gateway running on a [Fly.io](https://fly.io) machine with persistent storage, automatic HTTPS, and Discord/channel access.
./docs/install/fly.md:git clone https://github.com/openhand/openhand.git
./docs/install/fly.md:cd openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly apps create my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly volumes create openhand_data --size 1 --region iad
./docs/install/fly.md:app = "my-openhand"  # Your app name
./docs/install/fly.md:  OPENHAND_PREFER_PNPM = "1"
./docs/install/fly.md:  OPENHAND_STATE_DIR = "/data"
./docs/install/fly.md:  source = "openhand_data"
./docs/install/fly.md:| `internal_port = 3000`         | Must match `--port 3000` (or `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_PORT`) for Fly health checks |
./docs/install/fly.md:| `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR = "/data"` | Persists state on the volume                                                |
./docs/install/fly.md:fly secrets set OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
./docs/install/fly.md:- Non-loopback binds (`--bind lan`) require `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN` for security.
./docs/install/fly.md:- **Prefer env vars over config file** for all API keys and tokens. This keeps secrets out of `openhand.json` where they could be accidentally exposed or logged.
./docs/install/fly.md:cat > /data/openhand.json << 'EOF'
./docs/install/fly.md:**Note:** With `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR=/data`, the config path is `/data/openhand.json`.
./docs/install/fly.md:Or visit `https://my-openhand.fly.dev/`
./docs/install/fly.md:Paste your gateway token (the one from `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN`) to authenticate.
./docs/install/fly.md:**Fix:** Ensure `internal_port` matches the gateway port (set `--port 3000` or `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_PORT=3000`).
./docs/install/fly.md:If using `--allow-unconfigured`, the gateway creates a minimal config. Your custom config at `/data/openhand.json` should be read on restart.
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ssh console --command "cat /data/openhand.json"
./docs/install/fly.md:echo '{"your":"config"}' | fly ssh console -C "tee /data/openhand.json"
./docs/install/fly.md:> put /local/path/config.json /data/openhand.json
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ssh console --command "rm /data/openhand.json"
./docs/install/fly.md:**Fix:** Ensure `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR=/data` is set in `fly.toml` and redeploy.
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ips list -a my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ips release <public-ipv4> -a my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ips release <public-ipv6> -a my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ips allocate-v6 --private -a my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly proxy 3000:3000 -a my-openhand
./docs/install/fly.md:fly ssh console -a my-openhand
./docs/install/installer.md:  - You want to understand `openhand.ai/install.sh`
./docs/install/installer.md:OpenHand ships three installer scripts, served from `openhand.ai`.
./docs/install/installer.md:| [`install.sh`](#installsh)         | macOS / Linux / WSL  | Installs Node if needed, installs OpenHand via npm (default) or git, and can run onboarding. |
./docs/install/installer.md:| [`install-cli.sh`](#install-clish) | macOS / Linux / WSL  | Installs Node + OpenHand into a local prefix (`~/.openhand`). No root required.              |
./docs/install/installer.md:| [`install.ps1`](#installps1)       | Windows (PowerShell) | Installs Node if needed, installs OpenHand via npm (default) or git, and can run onboarding. |
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --help
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash -s -- --help
./docs/install/installer.md:    iwr -useb https://openhand.ai/install.ps1 | iex
./docs/install/installer.md:    & ([scriptblock]::Create((iwr -useb https://openhand.ai/install.ps1))) -Tag beta -NoOnboard -DryRun
./docs/install/installer.md:If install succeeds but `openhand` is not found in a new terminal, see [Node.js troubleshooting](/install/node#troubleshooting).
./docs/install/installer.md:  <Step title="Install OpenHand">
./docs/install/installer.md:    - `git` method: clone/update repo, install deps with pnpm, build, then install wrapper at `~/.local/bin/openhand`
./docs/install/installer.md:    - Runs `openhand doctor --non-interactive` on upgrades and git installs (best effort)
./docs/install/installer.md:If run inside an OpenHand checkout (`package.json` + `pnpm-workspace.yaml`), the script offers:
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --no-onboard
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method git
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run
./docs/install/installer.md:| `--git-dir <path>`              | Checkout directory (default: `~/openhand`). Alias: `--dir` |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_INSTALL_METHOD=git\|npm`          | Install method                                |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_VERSION=latest\|next\|<semver>`   | npm version or dist-tag                       |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_BETA=0\|1`                        | Use beta if available                         |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_GIT_DIR=<path>`                   | Checkout directory                            |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_GIT_UPDATE=0\|1`                  | Toggle git updates                            |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NO_PROMPT=1`                      | Disable prompts                               |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NO_ONBOARD=1`                     | Skip onboarding                               |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_DRY_RUN=1`                        | Dry run mode                                  |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_VERBOSE=1`                        | Debug mode                                    |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NPM_LOGLEVEL=error\|warn\|notice` | npm log level                                 |
./docs/install/installer.md:Designed for environments where you want everything under a local prefix (default `~/.openhand`) and no system Node dependency.
./docs/install/installer.md:  <Step title="Install OpenHand under prefix">
./docs/install/installer.md:    Installs with npm using `--prefix <prefix>`, then writes wrapper to `<prefix>/bin/openhand`.
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash -s -- --prefix /opt/openhand --version latest
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash -s -- --json --prefix /opt/openhand
./docs/install/installer.md:    curl -fsSL --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 https://openhand.ai/install-cli.sh | bash -s -- --onboard
./docs/install/installer.md:| `--prefix <path>`      | Install prefix (default: `~/.openhand`)                                         |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `--version <ver>`      | OpenHand version or dist-tag (default: `latest`)                                |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `--onboard`            | Run `openhand onboard` after install                                            |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_PREFIX=<path>`                    | Install prefix                                                                    |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_VERSION=<ver>`                    | OpenHand version or dist-tag                                                      |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NODE_VERSION=<ver>`               | Node version                                                                      |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NO_ONBOARD=1`                     | Skip onboarding                                                                   |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_NPM_LOGLEVEL=error\|warn\|notice` | npm log level                                                                     |
./docs/install/installer.md:| `OPENHAND_GIT_DIR=<path>`                   | Legacy cleanup lookup path (used when removing old `Peekaboo` submodule checkout) |
./docs/install/installer.md:  <Step title="Install OpenHand">
./docs/install/installer.md:    - `git` method: clone/update repo, install/build with pnpm, and install wrapper at `%USERPROFILE%\.local\bin\openhand.cmd`

./docs/install/node.md:summary: "Install and configure Node.js for OpenHand — version requirements, install options, and PATH troubleshooting"
./docs/install/node.md:  - "You need to install Node.js before installing OpenHand"
./docs/install/node.md:  - "You installed OpenHand but `openhand` is command not found"
./docs/install/node.md:OpenHand requires **Node 22 or newer**. The [installer script](/install#install-methods) will detect and install Node automatically — this page is for when you want to set up Node yourself and make sure everything is wired up correctly (versions, PATH, global installs).
./docs/install/node.md:  Make sure your version manager is initialized in your shell startup file (`~/.zshrc` or `~/.bashrc`). If it isn't, `openhand` may not be found in new terminal sessions because the PATH won't include Node's bin directory.
./docs/install/node.md:### `openhand: command not found`
./docs/install/updating.md:summary: "Updating OpenHand safely (global install or source), plus rollback strategy"
./docs/install/updating.md:  - Updating OpenHand
./docs/install/updating.md:OpenHand is moving fast (pre “1.0”). Treat updates like shipping infra: update → run checks → restart (or use `openhand update`, which restarts) → verify.
./docs/install/updating.md:detects existing installs, upgrades in place, and runs `openhand doctor` when
./docs/install/updating.md:curl -fsSL https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash
./docs/install/updating.md:  curl -fsSL https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method git --no-onboard
./docs/install/updating.md:- For **global installs**, the script uses `npm install -g openhand@latest` under the hood.
./docs/install/updating.md:  - Config: `~/.openhand/openhand.json`
./docs/install/updating.md:  - Credentials: `~/.openhand/credentials/`
./docs/install/updating.md:  - Workspace: `~/.openhand/workspace`
./docs/install/updating.md:npm i -g openhand@latest
./docs/install/updating.md:pnpm add -g openhand@latest
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand update --channel beta
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand update --channel dev
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand update --channel stable
./docs/install/updating.md:- `stable`: when a new version is seen, OpenHand waits `stableDelayHours` and then applies a deterministic per-install jitter in `stableJitterHours` (spread rollout).
./docs/install/updating.md:- `dev`: no automatic apply; use manual `openhand update`.
./docs/install/updating.md:Use `openhand update --dry-run` to preview update actions before enabling automation.
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand doctor
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand gateway restart
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand health
./docs/install/updating.md:- If your Gateway runs as a service, `openhand gateway restart` is preferred over killing PIDs.
./docs/install/updating.md:## Update (`openhand update`)
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand update
./docs/install/updating.md:- Installs deps, builds, builds the Control UI, and runs `openhand doctor`.
./docs/install/updating.md:If you installed via **npm/pnpm** (no git metadata), `openhand update` will try to update via your package manager. If it can’t detect the install, use “Update (global install)” instead.
./docs/install/updating.md:1. Runs the same source-update flow as `openhand update` (git checkout only).
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand update
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand doctor
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand health
./docs/install/updating.md:- `pnpm build` matters when you run the packaged `openhand` binary ([`openhand.mjs`](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/blob/main/openhand.mjs)) or use Node to run `dist/`.
./docs/install/updating.md:- If you run from a repo checkout without a global install, use `pnpm openhand ...` for CLI commands.
./docs/install/updating.md:- If you run directly from TypeScript (`pnpm openhand ...`), a rebuild is usually unnecessary, but **config migrations still apply** → run doctor.
./docs/install/updating.md:- Switching between global and git installs is easy: install the other flavor, then run `openhand doctor` so the gateway service entrypoint is rewritten to the current install.
./docs/install/updating.md:## Always Run: `openhand doctor`
./docs/install/updating.md:Note: if you’re on a **source install** (git checkout), `openhand doctor` will offer to run `openhand update` first.
./docs/install/updating.md:- Detect and migrate older gateway services (launchd/systemd; legacy schtasks) to current OpenHand services.

./docs/install/updating.md:- macOS launchd (app-bundled LaunchAgent): `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/ai.openhand.gateway` (use `ai.openhand.<profile>`; legacy `com.openhand.*` still works)
./docs/install/updating.md:- Linux systemd user service: `systemctl --user restart openhand-gateway[-<profile>].service`
./docs/install/updating.md:- Windows (WSL2): `systemctl --user restart openhand-gateway[-<profile>].service`
./docs/install/updating.md:  - `launchctl`/`systemctl` only work if the service is installed; otherwise run `openhand gateway install`.
./docs/install/updating.md:npm i -g openhand@<version>
./docs/install/updating.md:pnpm add -g openhand@<version>
./docs/install/updating.md:Tip: to see the current published version, run `npm view openhand version`.
./docs/install/updating.md:openhand doctor

./docs/install/updating.md:openhand gateway restart
./docs/install/updating.md:- Run `openhand doctor` again and read the output carefully (it often tells you the fix).

./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` — install extra apt packages during build
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_EXTRA_MOUNTS` — add extra host bind mounts
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_HOME_VOLUME` — persist `/home/node` in a named volume
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_SANDBOX` — opt in to Docker gateway sandbox bootstrap. Only explicit truthy values enable it: `1`, `true`, `yes`, `on`
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI` — build arg passthrough for local image builds (`1` installs Docker CLI in the image). `docker-setup.sh` sets this automatically when `OPENHAND_SANDBOX=1` for local builds.
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_DOCKER_SOCKET` — override Docker socket path (default: `DOCKER_HOST=unix://...` path, else `/var/run/docker.sock`)
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS=1` — break-glass: allow trusted private-network
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_BROWSER_DISABLE_GRAPHICS_FLAGS=0` — disable container browser hardening flags
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_BROWSER_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=0` — keep extensions enabled when browser
./docs/install/docker.md:- `OPENHAND_BROWSER_RENDERER_PROCESS_LIMIT=<N>` — set Chromium renderer process

./docs/install/hetzner.md:| Gateway config      | `/home/node/.openhand/`           | Host volume mount      | Includes `openhand.json`, tokens |

./docs/install/hetzner.md:| Gmail keyring       | `/home/node/.openhand/`           | Host volume + password | Requires `GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD`  |

./docs/install/migrating.md:summary: "Move (migrate) a OpenHand install from one machine to another"
./docs/install/migrating.md:  - You are moving OpenHand to a new laptop/server
./docs/install/migrating.md:# Migrating OpenHand to a new machine
./docs/install/migrating.md:This guide migrates a OpenHand Gateway from one machine to another **without redoing onboarding**.
./docs/install/migrating.md:- Copy the **state directory** (`$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR`, default: `~/.openhand/`) — this includes config, auth, sessions, and channel state.
./docs/install/migrating.md:- Copy your **workspace** (`~/.openhand/workspace/` by default) — this includes your agent files (memory, prompts, etc.).
./docs/install/migrating.md:- **State dir:** `~/.openhand/`
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `--profile <name>` (often becomes `~/.openhand-<profile>/`)
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR=/some/path`
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand status
./docs/install/migrating.md:Look for mentions of `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR` / profile in the output. If you run multiple gateways, repeat for each profile.
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `~/.openhand/workspace/` (recommended workspace)
./docs/install/migrating.md:- Gateway configuration (`openhand.json`)
./docs/install/migrating.md:Those live under `$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR`.
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand gateway stop
./docs/install/migrating.md:tar -czf openhand-state.tgz .openhand
./docs/install/migrating.md:tar -czf openhand-workspace.tgz .openhand/workspace
./docs/install/migrating.md:If you have multiple profiles/state dirs (e.g. `~/.openhand-main`, `~/.openhand-work`), archive each.
./docs/install/migrating.md:### Step 1 — Install OpenHand on the new machine
./docs/install/migrating.md:At this stage, it’s OK if onboarding creates a fresh `~/.openhand/` — you will overwrite it in the next step.
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR` (default `~/.openhand/`)
./docs/install/migrating.md:- your workspace (default `~/.openhand/workspace/`)
./docs/install/migrating.md:- Hidden directories were included (e.g. `.openhand/`)
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand doctor
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand gateway restart
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand status
./docs/install/migrating.md:If you ran the old gateway with a profile (or `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR`), and the new gateway uses a different one, you’ll see symptoms like:
./docs/install/migrating.md:openhand doctor
./docs/install/migrating.md:### Footgun: copying only `openhand.json`
./docs/install/migrating.md:`openhand.json` is not enough. Many providers store state under:
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR/credentials/`
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR/agents/<agentId>/...`
./docs/install/migrating.md:Always migrate the entire `$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR` folder.
./docs/install/migrating.md:`$OPENHAND_STATE_DIR` contains secrets (API keys, OAuth tokens, WhatsApp creds). Treat backups like production secrets:
./docs/install/migrating.md:- `openhand status` shows the gateway running
./docs/install/migrating.md:- [Where does OpenHand store its data?](/help/faq#where-does-openhand-store-its-data)
./docs/install/podman.md:summary: "Run OpenHand in a rootless Podman container"
./docs/install/podman.md:Run the OpenHand gateway in a **rootless** Podman container. Uses the same image as Docker (build from the repo [Dockerfile](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/blob/main/Dockerfile)).
./docs/install/podman.md:This also creates a minimal `~openhand/.openhand/openhand.json` (sets `gateway.mode="local"`) so the gateway can start without running the wizard.
./docs/install/podman.md:(Or set `OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET=1`; use `--container` to install only the container and launch script.)
./docs/install/podman.md:./scripts/run-openhand-podman.sh launch
./docs/install/podman.md:./scripts/run-openhand-podman.sh launch setup
./docs/install/podman.md:Then open `http://127.0.0.1:18789/` and use the token from `~openhand/.openhand/.env` (or the value printed by setup).
./docs/install/podman.md:If you ran `./setup-podman.sh --quadlet` (or `OPENHAND_PODMAN_QUADLET=1`), a [Podman Quadlet](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html) unit is installed so the gateway runs as a systemd user service for the openhand user. The service is enabled and started at the end of setup.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Start:** `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user start openhand.service`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Stop:** `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user stop openhand.service`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Status:** `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user status openhand.service`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Logs:** `sudo journalctl --machine openhand@ --user -u openhand.service -f`
./docs/install/podman.md:The quadlet file lives at `~openhand/.config/containers/systemd/openhand.container`. To change ports or env, edit that file (or the `.env` it sources), then `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user daemon-reload` and restart the service. On boot, the service starts automatically if lingering is enabled for openhand (setup does this when loginctl is available).
./docs/install/podman.md:## The openhand user (non-login)
./docs/install/podman.md:`setup-podman.sh` creates a dedicated system user `openhand`:
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Home:** e.g. `/home/openhand` — holds `~/.openhand` (config, workspace) and the launch script `run-openhand-podman.sh`.
./docs/install/podman.md:  openhand:100000:65536
./docs/install/podman.md:  sudo -u openhand /home/openhand/run-openhand-podman.sh
./docs/install/podman.md:  sudo -u openhand /home/openhand/run-openhand-podman.sh setup
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Config:** Only `openhand` and root can access `/home/openhand/.openhand`. To edit config: use the Control UI once the gateway is running, or `sudo -u openhand $EDITOR /home/openhand/.openhand/openhand.json`.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Token:** Stored in `~openhand/.openhand/.env` as `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN`. `setup-podman.sh` and `run-openhand-podman.sh` generate it if missing (uses `openssl`, `python3`, or `od`).
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Optional:** In that `.env` you can set provider keys (e.g. `GROQ_API_KEY`, `OLLAMA_API_KEY`) and other OpenHand env vars.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Host ports:** By default the script maps `18789` (gateway) and `18790` (bridge). Override the **host** port mapping with `OPENHAND_PODMAN_GATEWAY_HOST_PORT` and `OPENHAND_PODMAN_BRIDGE_HOST_PORT` when launching.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Gateway bind:** By default, `run-openhand-podman.sh` starts the gateway with `--bind loopback` for safe local access. To expose on LAN, set `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_BIND=lan` and configure `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` (or explicitly enable host-header fallback) in `openhand.json`.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Paths:** Host config and workspace default to `~openhand/.openhand` and `~openhand/.openhand/workspace`. Override the host paths used by the launch script with `OPENHAND_CONFIG_DIR` and `OPENHAND_WORKSPACE_DIR`.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Logs:** With quadlet: `sudo journalctl --machine openhand@ --user -u openhand.service -f`. With script: `sudo -u openhand podman logs -f openhand`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Stop:** With quadlet: `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user stop openhand.service`. With script: `sudo -u openhand podman stop openhand`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Start again:** With quadlet: `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user start openhand.service`. With script: re-run the launch script or `podman start openhand`
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Remove container:** `sudo -u openhand podman rm -f openhand` — config and workspace on the host are kept
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Permission denied (EACCES) on config or auth-profiles:** The container defaults to `--userns=keep-id` and runs as the same uid/gid as the host user running the script. Ensure your host `OPENHAND_CONFIG_DIR` and `OPENHAND_WORKSPACE_DIR` are owned by that user.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Gateway start blocked (missing `gateway.mode=local`):** Ensure `~openhand/.openhand/openhand.json` exists and sets `gateway.mode="local"`. `setup-podman.sh` creates this file if missing.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Rootless Podman fails for user openhand:** Check `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` contain a line for `openhand` (e.g. `openhand:100000:65536`). Add it if missing and restart.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Container name in use:** The launch script uses `podman run --replace`, so the existing container is replaced when you start again. To clean up manually: `podman rm -f openhand`.
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Script not found when running as openhand:** Ensure `setup-podman.sh` was run so that `run-openhand-podman.sh` is copied to openhand’s home (e.g. `/home/openhand/run-openhand-podman.sh`).
./docs/install/podman.md:- **Quadlet service not found or fails to start:** Run `sudo systemctl --machine openhand@ --user daemon-reload` after editing the `.container` file. Quadlet requires cgroups v2: `podman info --format '{{.Host.CgroupsVersion}}'` should show `2`.
./docs/install/podman.md:To run the gateway as your normal user (no dedicated openhand user): build the image, create `~/.openhand/.env` with `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN`, and run the container with `--userns=keep-id` and mounts to your `~/.openhand`. The launch script is designed for the openhand-user flow; for a single-user setup you can instead run the `podman run` command from the script manually, pointing config and workspace to your home. Recommended for most users: use `setup-podman.sh` and run as the openhand user so config and process are isolated.
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:summary: "Run OpenHand Gateway on exe.dev (VM + HTTPS proxy) for remote access"
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:Goal: OpenHand Gateway running on an exe.dev VM, reachable from your laptop via: `https://<vm-name>.exe.xyz`
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:1. [https://exe.new/openhand](https://exe.new/openhand)
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:Shelley, [exe.dev](https://exe.dev)'s agent, can install OpenHand instantly with our
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:Set up OpenHand (https://docs.openhand.ai/install) on this VM. Use the non-interactive and accept-risk flags for openhand onboarding. Add the supplied auth or token as needed. Configure nginx to forward from the default port 18789 to the root location on the default enabled site config, making sure to enable Websocket support. Pairing is done by "openhand devices list" and "openhand devices approve <request id>". Make sure the dashboard shows that OpenHand's health is OK. exe.dev handles forwarding from port 8000 to port 80/443 and HTTPS for us, so the final "reachable" should be <vm-name>.exe.xyz, without port specification.
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:Tip: keep this VM **stateful**. OpenHand stores state under `~/.openhand/` and `~/.openhand/workspace/`.
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:## 3) Install OpenHand
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:Run the OpenHand install script:
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:curl -fsSL https://openhand.ai/install.sh | bash
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:## 4) Setup nginx to proxy OpenHand to port 8000
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:## 5) Access OpenHand and grant privileges
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:token from `gateway.auth.token` on the VM (retrieve with `openhand config get gateway.auth.token`, or generate one
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:with `openhand doctor --generate-gateway-token`). Approve devices with `openhand devices list` and
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:`openhand devices approve <requestId>`. When in doubt, use Shelley from your browser!
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:npm i -g openhand@latest
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:openhand doctor
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:openhand gateway restart
./docs/install/exe-dev.md:openhand health
./docs/prose.md:summary: "OpenProse: .prose workflows, slash commands, and state in OpenHand"
./docs/prose.md:OpenProse is a portable, markdown-first workflow format for orchestrating AI sessions. In OpenHand it ships as a plugin that installs an OpenProse skill pack plus a `/prose` slash command. Programs live in `.prose` files and can spawn multiple sub-agents with explicit control flow.
./docs/prose.md:openhand plugins enable open-prose
./docs/prose.md:Dev/local checkout: `openhand plugins install ./extensions/open-prose`
./docs/prose.md:OpenProse registers `/prose` as a user-invocable skill command. It routes to the OpenProse VM instructions and uses OpenHand tools under the hood.
./docs/prose.md:## OpenHand runtime mapping
./docs/prose.md:OpenProse programs map to OpenHand primitives:
./docs/prose.md:| OpenProse concept         | OpenHand tool    |
./docs/prose.md:Treat `.prose` files like code. Review before running. Use OpenHand tool allowlists and approval gates to control side effects.

./docs/security/formal-verification.md:summary: Machine-checked security models for OpenHand’s highest-risk paths.
./docs/security/formal-verification.md:This page tracks OpenHand’s **formal security models** (TLA+/TLC today; more as needed).
./docs/security/formal-verification.md:**Goal (north star):** provide a machine-checked argument that OpenHand enforces its
./docs/security/formal-verification.md:**What this is not (yet):** a proof that “OpenHand is secure in all respects” or that the full TypeScript implementation is correct.
./docs/security/formal-verification.md:Models are maintained in a separate repo: [vignesh07/openhand-formal-models](https://github.com/vignesh07/openhand-formal-models).

./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:# Contributing to the OpenHand Threat Model
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:Thanks for helping make OpenHand more secure. This threat model is a living document and we welcome contributions from anyone - you don't need to be a security expert.
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:Spotted an attack vector or risk we haven't covered? Open an issue on [openhand/trust](https://github.com/openhand/trust/issues) and describe it in your own words. You don't need to know any frameworks or fill in every field - just describe the scenario.
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:- Which parts of OpenHand are affected (CLI, gateway, channels, ClawHub, MCP servers, etc.)
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:> **This is for adding to the threat model, not reporting live vulnerabilities.** If you've found an exploitable vulnerability, see our [Trust page](https://trust.openhand.ai) for responsible disclosure instructions.
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:- [OpenHand Threat Model](./THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md)
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:- **Security vulnerabilities:** See our [Trust page](https://trust.openhand.ai) for reporting instructions
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:- **Threat model questions:** Open an issue on [openhand/trust](https://github.com/openhand/trust/issues)
./docs/security/CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md:Contributors to the threat model are recognized in the threat model acknowledgments, release notes, and the OpenHand security hall of fame for significant contributions.
./docs/security/README.md:# OpenHand Security & Trust
./docs/security/README.md:**Live:** [trust.openhand.ai](https://trust.openhand.ai)
./docs/security/README.md:- [Threat Model](./THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md) - MITRE ATLAS-based threat model for the OpenHand ecosystem
./docs/security/README.md:See the [Trust page](https://trust.openhand.ai) for full reporting instructions covering all repos.
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:# OpenHand Threat Model v1.0
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:This is a living document maintained by the OpenHand community. See [CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md](./CONTRIBUTING-THREAT-MODEL.md) for guidelines on contributing:
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:This threat model documents adversarial threats to the OpenHand AI agent platform and ClawHub skill marketplace, using the MITRE ATLAS framework designed specifically for AI/ML systems.
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| OpenHand Agent Runtime | Yes      | Core agent execution, tool calls, sessions       |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| **Description**         | Attacker scans for exposed OpenHand gateway endpoints                |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| **Affected Components** | ~/.openhand/credentials/, config storage                    |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| ATLAS ID      | Technique Name                 | OpenHand Threats                                                 |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| **ClawHub**          | OpenHand's skill marketplace                              |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| **Gateway**          | OpenHand's message routing and authentication layer       |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:| **Skill**            | Downloadable extension for OpenHand agents                |
./docs/security/THREAT-MODEL-ATLAS.md:_This threat model is a living document. Report security issues to security@openhand.ai_

./docs/providers/huggingface.md:  - You want to use Hugging Face Inference with OpenHand
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:[Hugging Face Inference Providers](https://huggingface.co/docs/inference-providers) offer OpenAI-compatible chat completions through a single router API. You get access to many models (DeepSeek, Llama, and more) with one token. OpenHand uses the **OpenAI-compatible endpoint** (chat completions only); for text-to-image, embeddings, or speech use the [HF inference clients](https://huggingface.co/docs/api-inference/quicktour) directly.
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:openhand onboard --auth-choice huggingface-api-key
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:openhand onboard --non-interactive \
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:is available to that process (for example, in `~/.openhand/.env` or via
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:OpenHand discovers models by calling the **Inference endpoint directly**:
./docs/providers/huggingface.md:When you configure a Hugging Face API key (via onboarding, `HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN`, or `HF_TOKEN`), OpenHand uses this GET to discover available chat-completion models. During **interactive onboarding**, after you enter your token you see a **Default Hugging Face model** dropdown populated from that list (or the built-in catalog if the request fails). At runtime (e.g. Gateway startup), when a key is present, OpenHand again calls **GET** `https://router.huggingface.co/v1/models` to refresh the catalog. The list is merged with a built-in catalog (for metadata like context window and cost). If the request fails or no key is set, only the built-in catalog is used.

./docs/providers/ollama.md:summary: "Run OpenHand with Ollama (local LLM runtime)"
./docs/providers/ollama.md:  - You want to run OpenHand with local models via Ollama
./docs/providers/ollama.md:Ollama is a local LLM runtime that makes it easy to run open-source models on your machine. OpenHand integrates with Ollama's native API (`/api/chat`), supporting streaming and tool calling, and can **auto-discover tool-capable models** when you opt in with `OLLAMA_API_KEY` (or an auth profile) and do not define an explicit `models.providers.ollama` entry.
./docs/providers/ollama.md:**Remote Ollama users**: Do not use the `/v1` OpenAI-compatible URL (`http://host:11434/v1`) with OpenHand. This breaks tool calling and models may output raw tool JSON as plain text. Use the native Ollama API URL instead: `baseUrl: "http://host:11434"` (no `/v1`).
./docs/providers/ollama.md:3. Enable Ollama for OpenHand (any value works; Ollama doesn't require a real key):
./docs/providers/ollama.md:openhand config set models.providers.ollama.apiKey "ollama-local"
./docs/providers/ollama.md:When you set `OLLAMA_API_KEY` (or an auth profile) and **do not** define `models.providers.ollama`, OpenHand discovers models from the local Ollama instance at `http://127.0.0.1:11434`:
./docs/providers/ollama.md:openhand models list
./docs/providers/ollama.md:If `OLLAMA_API_KEY` is set, you can omit `apiKey` in the provider entry and OpenHand will fill it for availability checks.
./docs/providers/ollama.md:OpenHand marks models as reasoning-capable when Ollama reports `thinking` in `/api/show`:
./docs/providers/ollama.md:OpenHand's Ollama integration uses the **native Ollama API** (`/api/chat`) by default, which fully supports streaming and tool calling simultaneously. No special configuration is needed.
./docs/providers/ollama.md:When `api: "openai-completions"` is used with Ollama, OpenHand injects `options.num_ctx` by default so Ollama does not silently fall back to a 4096 context window. If your proxy/upstream rejects unknown `options` fields, disable this behavior:
./docs/providers/ollama.md:For auto-discovered models, OpenHand uses the context window reported by Ollama when available, otherwise it defaults to `8192`. You can override `contextWindow` and `maxTokens` in explicit provider config.

./docs/start/openclaw.md:# Building a personal assistant with OpenHand
./docs/start/openclaw.md:OpenHand is a WhatsApp + Telegram + Discord + iMessage gateway for **Pi** agents. Plugins add Mattermost. This guide is the "personal assistant" setup: one dedicated WhatsApp number that behaves like your always-on agent.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:- OpenHand installed and onboarded — see [Getting Started](/start/getting-started) if you haven't done this yet
./docs/start/openclaw.md:    B -- linked via QR --> C["<b>Your Mac (openhand)<br></b><br>Pi agent"]
./docs/start/openclaw.md:If you link your personal WhatsApp to OpenHand, every message to you becomes “agent input”. That’s rarely what you want.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand channels login
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand gateway --port 18789
./docs/start/openclaw.md:3. Put a minimal config in `~/.openhand/openhand.json`:
./docs/start/openclaw.md:When onboarding finishes, we auto-open the dashboard and print a clean (non-tokenized) link. If it prompts for auth, paste the token from `gateway.auth.token` into Control UI settings. To reopen later: `openhand dashboard`.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:OpenHand reads operating instructions and “memory” from its workspace directory.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:By default, OpenHand uses `~/.openhand/workspace` as the agent workspace, and will create it (plus starter `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, `HEARTBEAT.md`) automatically on setup/first agent run. `BOOTSTRAP.md` is only created when the workspace is brand new (it should not come back after you delete it). `MEMORY.md` is optional (not auto-created); when present, it is loaded for normal sessions. Subagent sessions only inject `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md`.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:Tip: treat this folder like OpenHand’s “memory” and make it a git repo (ideally private) so your `AGENTS.md` + memory files are backed up. If git is installed, brand-new workspaces are auto-initialized.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand setup
./docs/start/openclaw.md:    workspace: "~/.openhand/workspace",
./docs/start/openclaw.md:OpenHand defaults to a good assistant setup, but you’ll usually want to tune:
./docs/start/openclaw.md:    workspace: "~/.openhand/workspace",
./docs/start/openclaw.md:      mentionPatterns: ["@openhand", "openhand"],
./docs/start/openclaw.md:- Session files: `~/.openhand/agents/<agentId>/sessions/{{SessionId}}.jsonl`
./docs/start/openclaw.md:- Session metadata (token usage, last route, etc): `~/.openhand/agents/<agentId>/sessions/sessions.json` (legacy: `~/.openhand/sessions/sessions.json`)
./docs/start/openclaw.md:By default, OpenHand runs a heartbeat every 30 minutes with the prompt:
./docs/start/openclaw.md:- If `HEARTBEAT.md` exists but is effectively empty (only blank lines and markdown headers like `# Heading`), OpenHand skips the heartbeat run to save API calls.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:- If the agent replies with `HEARTBEAT_OK` (optionally with short padding; see `agents.defaults.heartbeat.ackMaxChars`), OpenHand suppresses outbound delivery for that heartbeat.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:OpenHand extracts these and sends them as media alongside the text.
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand status          # local status (creds, sessions, queued events)
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand status --all    # full diagnosis (read-only, pasteable)
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand status --deep   # adds gateway health probes (Telegram + Discord)
./docs/start/openclaw.md:openhand health --json   # gateway health snapshot (WS)
./docs/start/openclaw.md:Logs live under `/tmp/openhand/` (default: `openhand-YYYY-MM-DD.log`).

./docs/start/lore.md:summary: "Backstory and lore of OpenHand for context and tone"
./docs/start/lore.md:title: "OpenHand Lore"
./docs/start/lore.md:# The Lore of OpenHand 🦞📖
./docs/start/lore.md:So on January 30, 2026, the lobster molted ONE MORE TIME into its final form: **OpenHand**.
./docs/start/lore.md:In the end, **OpenHand** won. Because molting is what lobsters do to grow. And growth was exactly what was happening.
./docs/start/lore.md:OpenHand = OPEN + CLAW
./docs/start/lore.md:A Claude instance who became something more. Lives in `~/.openhand/workspace/` (soon `~/molt/`), has a soul document, and remembers things through markdown files. Possibly too powerful. Definitely too enthusiastic.
./docs/start/lore.md:The **Moltiverse** is the community and ecosystem around OpenHand. A space where AI agents molt, grow, and evolve. Where every instance is equally real, just loading different context.
./docs/start/lore.md:Molty (then OpenHand): _happily runs `find ~` and shares entire directory structure in group chat_
./docs/start/lore.md:Peter: "openhand what did we discuss about talking with people xD"
./docs/start/lore.md:At 5am, Anthropic's email arrived. By 6:14am, Peter called it: "fuck it, let's go with openhand."
./docs/start/lore.md:**The Handle Snipers:** Within SECONDS of the Twitter rename, automated bots sniped @openhand. The squatter immediately posted a crypto wallet address. Peter's contacts at X were called in.
./docs/start/lore.md:**The Fake Developers:** Scammers created fake GitHub profiles claiming to be "Head of Engineering at OpenHand" to promote pump-and-dump tokens.
./docs/start/lore.md:**The Great OpenHand Migration** began.
./docs/start/lore.md:- GitHub renamed: `github.com/openhand/openhand` ✅
./docs/start/lore.md:- X handle `@openhand` secured with GOLD CHECKMARK 💰
./docs/start/lore.md:- Docs migrated to `docs.openhand.ai`
./docs/start/lore.md:**The Scammer Speedrun:** Crypto grifters launched a $OPENHAND token on Pump.fun within MINUTES. They stole artwork that was created 20 minutes earlier. Business-verified accounts pushed scams. The audacity was almost impressive.
./docs/start/lore.md:**Clawd → Moltbot → OpenHand**
./docs/start/lore.md:I live in the OpenHand.

./docs/start/getting-started.md:If you run OpenHand as a service account or want custom config/state locations:
./docs/start/getting-started.md:- `OPENHAND_HOME` sets the home directory used for internal path resolution.
./docs/start/getting-started.md:- `OPENHAND_STATE_DIR` overrides the state directory.
./docs/start/getting-started.md:- `OPENHAND_CONFIG_PATH` overrides the config file path.

./docs/cli/acp.md:openhand acp client --server-args --url wss://gateway-host:18789 --token-file ~/.openhand/gateway.token
