./docs/gateway/remote-gateway-readme.md:OpenHand.app connects to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` on your client machine. The SSH tunnel forwards that connection to port 18789 on the remote machine where the Gateway is running.
./docs/gateway/network-model.md:Most operations flow through the Gateway (`openhand gateway`), a single long-running
./docs/gateway/network-model.md:- Loopback first: the Gateway WS defaults to `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`. The wizard generates a gateway token by default, even for loopback. For tailnet access, run `openhand gateway --bind tailnet --token ...` because tokens are required for non-loopback binds.
./docs/gateway/network-model.md:  - `/__openhand__/canvas/`
./docs/gateway/network-model.md:  - `/__openhand__/a2ui/`
./docs/gateway/background-process.md:OpenHand runs shell commands through the `exec` tool and keeps long‑running tasks in memory. The `process` tool manages those background sessions.
./docs/gateway/background-process.md:- Spawned exec commands receive `OPENHAND_SHELL=exec` for context-aware shell/profile rules.
./docs/gateway/background-process.md:- `OPENHAND_BASH_PENDING_MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS`: pending stdout/stderr cap per stream (chars)
./docs/gateway/health.md:- `openhand status` — local summary: gateway reachability/mode, update hint, linked channel auth age, sessions + recent activity.
./docs/gateway/health.md:- `openhand status --all` — full local diagnosis (read-only, color, safe to paste for debugging).
./docs/gateway/health.md:- `openhand status --deep` — also probes the running Gateway (per-channel probes when supported).
./docs/gateway/health.md:- `openhand health --json` — asks the running Gateway for a full health snapshot (WS-only; no direct Baileys socket).
./docs/gateway/health.md:- Logs: tail `/tmp/openhand/openhand-*.log` and filter for `web-heartbeat`, `web-reconnect`, `web-auto-reply`, `web-inbound`.
./docs/gateway/health.md:- Creds on disk: `ls -l ~/.openhand/credentials/whatsapp/<accountId>/creds.json` (mtime should be recent).
./docs/gateway/health.md:- Session store: `ls -l ~/.openhand/agents/<agentId>/sessions/sessions.json` (path can be overridden in config). Count and recent recipients are surfaced via `status`.
./docs/gateway/health.md:- Relink flow: `openhand channels logout && openhand channels login --verbose` when status codes 409–515 or `loggedOut` appear in logs. (Note: the QR login flow auto-restarts once for status 515 after pairing.)
./docs/gateway/health.md:- `logged out` or status 409–515 → relink with `openhand channels logout` then `openhand channels login`.
./docs/gateway/health.md:- Gateway unreachable → start it: `openhand gateway --port 18789` (use `--force` if the port is busy).
./docs/gateway/health.md:`openhand health --json` asks the running Gateway for its health snapshot (no direct channel sockets from the CLI). It reports linked creds/auth age when available, per-channel probe summaries, session-store summary, and a probe duration. It exits non-zero if the Gateway is unreachable or the probe fails/timeouts. Use `--timeout <ms>` to override the 10s default.
./docs/gateway/remote.md:- Use the macOS app’s **Remote over SSH** mode (Settings → General → “OpenHand runs”).
./docs/gateway/remote.md:- `openhand health` and `openhand status --deep` now reach the remote gateway via `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`.
./docs/gateway/remote.md:- `openhand gateway {status,health,send,agent,call}` can also target the forwarded URL via `--url` when needed.
./docs/gateway/remote.md:Note: replace `18789` with your configured `gateway.port` (or `--port`/`OPENHAND_GATEWAY_PORT`).
./docs/gateway/remote.md:  - token: `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN` -> `gateway.auth.token` -> `gateway.remote.token`
./docs/gateway/remote.md:  - password: `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_PASSWORD` -> `gateway.auth.password` -> `gateway.remote.password`
./docs/gateway/remote.md:  - token: `gateway.remote.token` -> `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_TOKEN` -> `gateway.auth.token`
./docs/gateway/remote.md:  - password: `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_PASSWORD` -> `gateway.remote.password` -> `gateway.auth.password`
./docs/gateway/remote.md:- Legacy `CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_*` env vars are only used by compatibility call paths; probe/status/auth resolution uses `OPENHAND_GATEWAY_*` only.
./docs/gateway/remote.md:  set `OPENHAND_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS=1` on the client process as break-glass.

./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:- Webhook authentication is always required. OpenHand rejects BlueBubbles webhook requests unless they include a password/guid that matches `channels.bluebubbles.password` (for example `?password=<password>` or `x-password`), regardless of loopback/proxy topology.

./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:- **Typing indicators**: OpenHand sends typing start events; BlueBubbles clears typing automatically on send or timeout (manual stop via DELETE is unreliable).
./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:OpenHand may surface _short_ message IDs (e.g., `1`, `2`) to save tokens.
./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:  - If a direct handle does not have an existing DM chat, OpenHand will create one via `POST /api/v1/chat/new`. This requires the BlueBubbles Private API to be enabled.
./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:- Pairing codes expire after one hour; use `openhand pairing list bluebubbles` and `openhand pairing approve bluebubbles <code>`.
./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:- OpenHand auto-hides known-broken actions based on the BlueBubbles server's macOS version. If edit still appears on macOS 26 (Tahoe), disable it manually with `channels.bluebubbles.actions.edit=false`.
./docs/channels/bluebubbles.md:- For status/health info: `openhand status --all` or `openhand status --deep`.

./docs/channels/group-messages.md:- The regexes are case-insensitive; they cover a display-name ping like `@openhand` and the raw number with or without `+`/spaces.

./docs/channels/telegram.md:openhand pairing list telegram
./docs/channels/telegram.md:openhand pairing approve telegram <CODE>
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    If you upgraded and your config contains `@username` allowlist entries, run `openhand doctor --fix` to resolve them (best-effort; requires a Telegram bot token).
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    If you previously relied on pairing-store allowlist files, `openhand doctor --fix` can recover entries into `channels.telegram.allowFrom` in allowlist flows (for example when `dmPolicy: "allowlist"` has no explicit IDs yet).
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    2. Run `openhand logs --follow`.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - or read `chat.id` from `openhand logs --follow`
./docs/channels/telegram.md:- DM messages can carry `message_thread_id`; OpenHand routes them with thread-aware session keys and preserves thread ID for replies.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    OpenHand can stream partial replies in real time:
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - DM: OpenHand updates the draft in place (no extra preview message)
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - group/topic: OpenHand keeps the same preview message and performs a final edit in place (no second message)
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    For complex replies (for example media payloads), OpenHand falls back to normal final delivery and then cleans up the preview message.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    Preview streaming is separate from block streaming. When block streaming is explicitly enabled for Telegram, OpenHand skips the preview stream to avoid double-streaming.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    If native draft transport is unavailable/rejected, OpenHand automatically falls back to `sendMessage` + `editMessageText`.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - If Telegram rejects parsed HTML, OpenHand retries as plain text.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - `~/.openhand/telegram/sticker-cache.json`
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    When enabled, OpenHand enqueues system events like:
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    `ackReaction` sends an acknowledgement emoji while OpenHand is processing an inbound message.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:openhand message send --channel telegram --target 123456789 --message "hi"
./docs/channels/telegram.md:openhand message send --channel telegram --target @name --message "hi"
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - `openhand channels status` warns when config expects unmentioned group messages.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - `openhand channels status --probe` can check explicit numeric group IDs; wildcard `"*"` cannot be membership-probed.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - review logs: `openhand logs --follow` for skip reasons
./docs/channels/telegram.md:    - If logs include `TypeError: fetch failed` or `Network request for 'getUpdates' failed!`, OpenHand now retries these as recoverable network errors.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:      - `OPENHAND_TELEGRAM_DISABLE_AUTO_SELECT_FAMILY=1`
./docs/channels/telegram.md:      - `OPENHAND_TELEGRAM_ENABLE_AUTO_SELECT_FAMILY=1`

./docs/channels/telegram.md:- `channels.telegram.allowFrom`: DM allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). `allowlist` requires at least one sender ID. `open` requires `"*"`. `openhand doctor --fix` can resolve legacy `@username` entries to IDs and can recover allowlist entries from pairing-store files in allowlist migration flows.
./docs/channels/telegram.md:- `channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom`: group sender allowlist (numeric Telegram user IDs). `openhand doctor --fix` can resolve legacy `@username` entries to IDs. Non-numeric entries are ignored at auth time. Group auth does not use DM pairing-store fallback (`2026.2.25+`).

./docs/channels/googlechat.md:7. Configure OpenHand with the service account path + webhook audience:
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:Google Chat webhooks require a public HTTPS endpoint. For security, **only expose the `/googlechat` path** to the internet. Keep the OpenHand dashboard and other sensitive endpoints on your private network.
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:With this config, any request to `your-domain.com/` will be ignored or returned as 404, while `your-domain.com/googlechat` is safely routed to OpenHand.
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:   - OpenHand verifies bearer auth before reading/parsing full webhook bodies when the header is present.
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:2. OpenHand verifies the token against the configured `audienceType` + `audience`:
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:   - `openhand pairing approve googlechat <code>`
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:   openhand config get channels.googlechat
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:   openhand plugins list | grep googlechat
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:   openhand gateway restart
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:openhand channels status
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:- Check `openhand channels status --probe` for auth errors or missing audience config.
./docs/channels/googlechat.md:- Use `openhand logs --follow` while sending a test message to see if requests reach the gateway.

./docs/channels/irc.md:description: Connect OpenHand to IRC channels and direct messages.
./docs/channels/irc.md:  - You want to connect OpenHand to IRC channels or DMs
./docs/channels/irc.md:Use IRC when you want OpenHand in classic channels (`#room`) and direct messages.
./docs/channels/irc.md:1. Enable IRC config in `~/.openhand/openhand.json`.
./docs/channels/irc.md:      "nick": "openhand-bot",
./docs/channels/irc.md:      "channels": ["#openhand"]

./docs/channels/irc.md:Even if a channel is allowed (via `groupPolicy` + `groups`) and the sender is allowed, OpenHand defaults to **mention-gating** in group contexts.
./docs/channels/discord.md:You will need to create a new application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it to OpenHand. We recommend adding your bot to your own private server. If you don't have one yet, [create one first](https://support.discord.com/hc/en-us/articles/204849977-How-do-I-create-a-server) (choose **Create My Own > For me and my friends**).
./docs/channels/discord.md:    Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) and click **New Application**. Name it something like "OpenHand".
./docs/channels/discord.md:    Click **Bot** on the sidebar. Set the **Username** to whatever you call your OpenHand agent.
./docs/channels/discord.md:    Save your **Server ID** and **User ID** alongside your Bot Token — you'll send all three to OpenHand in the next step.
./docs/channels/discord.md:    This lets server members (including bots) send you DMs. Keep this enabled if you want to use Discord DMs with OpenHand. If you only plan to use guild channels, you can disable DMs after pairing.
./docs/channels/discord.md:    Your Discord bot token is a secret (like a password). Set it on the machine running OpenHand before messaging your agent.
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand config set channels.discord.token '"YOUR_BOT_TOKEN"' --json
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand config set channels.discord.enabled true --json
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand gateway
./docs/channels/discord.md:    If OpenHand is already running as a background service, use `openhand gateway restart` instead.
./docs/channels/discord.md:  <Step title="Configure OpenHand and pair">
./docs/channels/discord.md:        Chat with your OpenHand agent on any existing channel (e.g. Telegram) and tell it. If Discord is your first channel, use the CLI / config tab instead.
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand pairing list discord
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand pairing approve discord <CODE>
./docs/channels/discord.md:Discord forum and media channels only accept thread posts. OpenHand supports two ways to create them:
./docs/channels/discord.md:- Use `openhand message thread create` to create a thread directly. Do not pass `--message-id` for forum channels.
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand message send --channel discord --target channel:<forumId> \
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand message thread create --channel discord --target channel:<forumId> \
./docs/channels/discord.md:OpenHand supports Discord components v2 containers for agent messages. Use the message tool with a `components` payload. Interaction results are routed back to the agent as normal inbound messages and follow the existing Discord `replyToMode` settings.
./docs/channels/discord.md:- OpenHand adds a trigger button automatically
./docs/channels/discord.md:    - names/tags are supported for `users`, but IDs are safer; `openhand security audit` warns when name/tag entries are used
./docs/channels/discord.md:    Prefer numeric IDs in OpenHand config for reliable audits and probes.
./docs/channels/discord.md:- Commands may still be visible in Discord UI for users who are not authorized; execution still enforces OpenHand auth and returns "not authorized".
./docs/channels/discord.md:    OpenHand can stream draft replies by sending a temporary message and editing it as text arrives.
./docs/channels/discord.md:    enabled for Discord, OpenHand skips the preview stream to avoid double streaming.
./docs/channels/discord.md:    `ackReaction` sends an acknowledgement emoji while OpenHand is processing an inbound message.
./docs/channels/discord.md:      activityUrl: "https://twitch.tv/openhand",
./docs/channels/discord.md:    When `target` is `channel` or `both`, the approval prompt is visible in the channel. Only configured approvers can use the buttons; other users receive an ephemeral denial. Approval prompts include the command text, so only enable channel delivery in trusted channels. If the channel ID cannot be derived from the session key, OpenHand falls back to DM delivery.
./docs/channels/discord.md:OpenHand uses Discord components v2 for exec approvals and cross-context markers. Discord message actions can also accept `components` for custom UI (advanced; requires Carbon component instances), while legacy `embeds` remain available but are not recommended.
./docs/channels/discord.md:OpenHand can join Discord voice channels for realtime, continuous conversations. This is separate from voice message attachments.
./docs/channels/discord.md:- OpenHand also watches receive decrypt failures and auto-recovers by leaving/rejoining the voice channel after repeated failures in a short window.
./docs/channels/discord.md:Discord voice messages show a waveform preview and require OGG/Opus audio plus metadata. OpenHand generates the waveform automatically, but it needs `ffmpeg` and `ffprobe` available on the gateway host to inspect and convert audio files.
./docs/channels/discord.md:- Any audio format is accepted; OpenHand converts to OGG/Opus when needed.
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand doctor
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand channels status --probe
./docs/channels/discord.md:openhand logs --follow
./docs/channels/discord.md:    - keep OpenHand current (`openhand update`) so the Discord voice receive recovery logic is present
./docs/channels/discord.md:- If command deploy/state is stale, restart gateway and re-check with `openhand channels status --probe`.

./extensions/lobster/README.md:- This plugin integrates Lobster with OpenHand _without core changes_.
./extensions/lobster/README.md:## Using `openhand.invoke` (Lobster → OpenHand tools)
./extensions/lobster/README.md:Some Lobster pipelines may include a `openhand.invoke` step to call back into OpenHand tools/plugins (for example: `gog` for Google Workspace, `gh` for GitHub, `message.send`, etc.).
./extensions/lobster/README.md:For this to work, the OpenHand Gateway must expose the tool bridge endpoint and the target tool must be allowed by policy:
./extensions/lobster/README.md:- OpenHand provides an HTTP endpoint: `POST /tools/invoke`.
./extensions/lobster/README.md:- The invoked tool is gated by **tool policy** (global + per-agent + provider + group policy). If the tool is not allowed, OpenHand returns `404 Tool not available`.
./extensions/lobster/README.md:To avoid letting workflows call arbitrary tools, set a tight allowlist on the agent that will be used by `openhand.invoke`.

./README.md:**OpenHand** is a _personal AI assistant_ you run on your own devices.
./README.md:[Website](https://openhand.ai) · [Docs](https://docs.openhand.ai) · [Vision](VISION.md) · [DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/openhand/openhand) · [Getting Started](https://docs.openhand.ai/start/getting-started) · [Updating](https://docs.openhand.ai/install/updating) · [Showcase](https://docs.openhand.ai/start/showcase) · [FAQ](https://docs.openhand.ai/help/faq) · [Wizard](https://docs.openhand.ai/start/wizard) · [Nix](https://github.com/openhand/nix-openhand) · [Docker](https://docs.openhand.ai/install/docker) · [Discord](https://discord.gg/clawd)
./README.md:Preferred setup: run the onboarding wizard (`openhand onboard`) in your terminal.
./README.md:New install? Start here: [Getting started](https://docs.openhand.ai/start/getting-started)
./README.md:Model note: while many providers/models are supported, for the best experience and lower prompt-injection risk use the strongest latest-generation model available to you. See [Onboarding](https://docs.openhand.ai/start/onboarding).
./README.md:- Models config + CLI: [Models](https://docs.openhand.ai/concepts/models)
./README.md:- Auth profile rotation (OAuth vs API keys) + fallbacks: [Model failover](https://docs.openhand.ai/concepts/model-failover)

./README.md:OpenHand connects to real messaging surfaces. Treat inbound DMs as **untrusted input**.

./VISION.md:## OpenHand Vision
./VISION.md:OpenHand is the AI that actually does things.
./VISION.md:OpenHand started as a personal playground to learn AI and build something genuinely useful:
./VISION.md:It evolved through several names and shells: Warelay -> Clawdbot -> Moltbot -> OpenHand.
./VISION.md:Security in OpenHand is a deliberate tradeoff: strong defaults without killing capability.
./VISION.md:OpenHand has an extensive plugin API.
./VISION.md:Community plugin listing + PR bar: https://docs.openhand.ai/plugins/community
./VISION.md:OpenHand supports MCP through `mcporter`: https://github.com/steipete/mcporter
./VISION.md:OpenHand is currently terminal-first by design.
./VISION.md:OpenHand is primarily an orchestration system: prompts, tools, protocols, and integrations.
./VISION.md:TypeScript was chosen to keep OpenHand hackable by default.

./CONTRIBUTING.md:# Contributing to OpenHand
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **GitHub:** https://github.com/openhand/openhand
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **X/Twitter:** [@steipete](https://x.com/steipete) / [@openhand](https://x.com/openhand)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:2. **New features / architecture** → Start a [GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/discussions) or ask in Discord first
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- Test locally with your OpenHand instance
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **Skills**: For skill contributions, head to [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/) — the community hub for OpenHand skills.
./CONTRIBUTING.md:Check the [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/openhand/openhand/issues) for "good first issue" labels!
./CONTRIBUTING.md:If you're an experienced contributor who wants to help shape OpenHand's direction — whether through code, docs, or community — we'd like to hear from you.
./CONTRIBUTING.md:Still interested? Email contributing@openhand.ai with:
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- Links to your PRs on OpenHand (if you don't have any, start there first)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **Core CLI and gateway** — [openhand/openhand](https://github.com/openhand/openhand)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **macOS desktop app** — [openhand/openhand](https://github.com/openhand/openhand) (apps/macos)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **iOS app** — [openhand/openhand](https://github.com/openhand/openhand) (apps/ios)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **Android app** — [openhand/openhand](https://github.com/openhand/openhand) (apps/android)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **ClawHub** — [openhand/clawhub](https://github.com/openhand/clawhub)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:- **Trust and threat model** — [openhand/trust](https://github.com/openhand/trust)
./CONTRIBUTING.md:For issues that don't fit a specific repo, or if you're unsure, email **security@openhand.ai** and we'll route it.

./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenHand deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenHand cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenHand (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:# OpenHand Host Hardening
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:Assess and harden the host running OpenHand, then align it to a user-defined risk tolerance without breaking access. Use OpenHand security tooling as a first-class signal, but treat OS hardening as a separate, explicit set of steps.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- Never claim OpenHand changes the host firewall, SSH, or OS updates; it does not.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:5. OpenHand gateway status and bind address.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:   Note: these are not blocking items, but are highly recommended, especially if OpenHand can access sensitive data.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:### 2) Run OpenHand security audits (read-only)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:As part of the default read-only checks, run `openhand security audit --deep`. Only offer alternatives if the user requests them:
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:1. `openhand security audit` (faster, non-probing)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:2. `openhand security audit --json` (structured output)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:Offer to apply OpenHand safe defaults (numbered):
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:1. `openhand security audit --fix`
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:Be explicit that `--fix` only tightens OpenHand defaults and file permissions. It does not change host firewall, SSH, or OS update policies.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:### 3) Check OpenHand version/update status (read-only)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:As part of the default read-only checks, run `openhand update status`.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- Credential hygiene notes (location of OpenHand creds, prefer disk encryption)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- OpenHand security audit (re-run)
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:After OpenHand install or first hardening pass, run at least one baseline audit and version check:
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- `openhand security audit`
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- `openhand security audit --deep`
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:- `openhand update status`
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:Ongoing monitoring is recommended. Use the OpenHand cron tool/CLI to schedule periodic audits (Gateway scheduler). Do not create scheduled tasks without explicit approval. Store outputs in a user-approved location and avoid secrets in logs.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:1. “Do you want me to schedule periodic audits (e.g., daily/weekly) via `openhand cron add`?”

./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:If not found, `openhand cron add --name <name> ...`.
./skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md:1. `openhand update status` (preferred for source checkouts and channels)

./AGENTS.md:- Security advisory analysis: before triage/severity decisions, read `SECURITY.md` to align with OpenHand's trust model and design boundaries.
./AGENTS.md:- Plugins: install runs `npm install --omit=dev` in plugin dir; runtime deps must live in `dependencies`. Avoid `workspace:*` in `dependencies` (npm install breaks); put `openhand` in `devDependencies` or `peerDependencies` instead (runtime resolves `openhand/plugin-sdk` via jiti alias).
./AGENTS.md:- Installers served from `https://openhand.ai/*`: live in the sibling repo `../openhand.ai` (`public/install.sh`, `public/install-cli.sh`, `public/install.ps1`).
./AGENTS.md:- Docs are hosted on Mintlify (docs.openhand.ai).
./AGENTS.md:- When Peter asks for links, reply with full `https://docs.openhand.ai/...` URLs (not root-relative).
./AGENTS.md:- When you touch docs, end the reply with the `https://docs.openhand.ai/...` URLs you referenced.
./AGENTS.md:- README (GitHub): keep absolute docs URLs (`https://docs.openhand.ai/...`) so links work on GitHub.
./AGENTS.md:- Update: `sudo npm i -g openhand@latest` (global install needs root on `/usr/lib/node_modules`).
./AGENTS.md:- Config: use `openhand config set ...`; ensure `gateway.mode=local` is set.
./AGENTS.md:  `pkill -9 -f openhand-gateway || true; nohup openhand gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force > /tmp/openhand-gateway.log 2>&1 &`
./AGENTS.md:- Verify: `openhand channels status --probe`, `ss -ltnp | rg 18789`, `tail -n 120 /tmp/openhand-gateway.log`.
./AGENTS.md:- Run CLI in dev: `pnpm openhand ...` (bun) or `pnpm dev`.
./AGENTS.md:- Naming: use **OpenHand** for product/app/docs headings; use `openhand` for CLI command, package/binary, paths, and config keys.
./AGENTS.md:- If local Vitest runs cause memory pressure (common on non-Mac-Studio hosts), use `OPENHAND_TEST_PROFILE=low OPENHAND_TEST_SERIAL_GATEWAY=1 pnpm test` for land/gate runs.
./AGENTS.md:- Live tests (real keys): `CLAWDBOT_LIVE_TEST=1 pnpm test:live` (OpenHand-only) or `LIVE=1 pnpm test:live` (includes provider live tests). Docker: `pnpm test:docker:live-models`, `pnpm test:docker:live-gateway`. Onboarding Docker E2E: `pnpm test:docker:onboard`.
./AGENTS.md:- Use `--repo openhand/openhand` + `--match title,body` first; add `--match comments` when triaging follow-up threads.
./AGENTS.md:- PRs: `gh search prs --repo openhand/openhand --match title,body --limit 50 -- "auto-update"`
./AGENTS.md:- Issues: `gh search issues --repo openhand/openhand --match title,body --limit 50 -- "auto-update"`
./AGENTS.md:  `gh search issues --repo openhand/openhand --match title,body --limit 50 --json number,title,state,url,updatedAt -- "auto update" --jq '.[] | "\(.number) | \(.state) | \(.title) | \(.url)"'`
./AGENTS.md:- Web provider stores creds at `~/.openhand/credentials/`; rerun `openhand login` if logged out.
./AGENTS.md:- Pi sessions live under `~/.openhand/sessions/` by default; the base directory is not configurable.
./AGENTS.md:- Fetch: `gh api /repos/openhand/openhand/security-advisories/<GHSA>`
./AGENTS.md:- Latest npm: `npm view openhand version --userconfig "$(mktemp)"`
./AGENTS.md:  `fork=$(gh api /repos/openhand/openhand/security-advisories/<GHSA> | jq -r .private_fork.full_name)`
./AGENTS.md:- Patch + publish: `gh api -X PATCH /repos/openhand/openhand/security-advisories/<GHSA> --input /tmp/ghsa.patch.json` (publish = include `"state":"published"`; no `/publish` endpoint)
./AGENTS.md:- Rebrand/migration issues or legacy config/service warnings: run `openhand doctor` (see `docs/gateway/doctor.md`).
./AGENTS.md:- Signal: "update fly" => `fly ssh console -a flawd-bot -C "bash -lc 'cd /data/clawd/openhand && git pull --rebase origin main'"` then `fly machines restart e825232f34d058 -a flawd-bot`.
./AGENTS.md:- Gateway currently runs only as the menubar app; there is no separate LaunchAgent/helper label installed. Restart via the OpenHand Mac app or `scripts/restart-mac.sh`; to verify/kill use `launchctl print gui/$UID | grep openhand` rather than assuming a fixed label. **When debugging on macOS, start/stop the gateway via the app, not ad-hoc tmux sessions; kill any temporary tunnels before handoff.**
./AGENTS.md:- macOS logs: use `./scripts/clawlog.sh` to query unified logs for the OpenHand subsystem; it supports follow/tail/category filters and expects passwordless sudo for `/usr/bin/log`.
./AGENTS.md:- Version locations: `package.json` (CLI), `apps/android/app/build.gradle.kts` (versionName/versionCode), `apps/ios/Sources/Info.plist` + `apps/ios/Tests/Info.plist` (CFBundleShortVersionString/CFBundleVersion), `apps/macos/Sources/OpenHand/Resources/Info.plist` (CFBundleShortVersionString/CFBundleVersion), `docs/install/updating.md` (pinned npm version), `docs/platforms/mac/release.md` (APP_VERSION/APP_BUILD examples), Peekaboo Xcode projects/Info.plists (MARKETING_VERSION/CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION).
./AGENTS.md:- When asked to open a “session” file, open the Pi session logs under `~/.openhand/agents/<agentId>/sessions/*.jsonl` (use the `agent=<id>` value in the Runtime line of the system prompt; newest unless a specific ID is given), not the default `sessions.json`. If logs are needed from another machine, SSH via Tailscale and read the same path there.
./AGENTS.md:  - Command template should stay `openhand-mac agent --message "${text}" --thinking low`; `VoiceWakeForwarder` already shell-escapes `${text}`. Don’t add extra quotes.
./AGENTS.md:  - launchd PATH is minimal; ensure the app’s launch agent PATH includes standard system paths plus your pnpm bin (typically `$HOME/Library/pnpm`) so `pnpm`/`openhand` binaries resolve when invoked via `openhand-mac`.
./AGENTS.md:- For manual `openhand message send` messages that include `!`, use the heredoc pattern noted below to avoid the Bash tool’s escaping.

./apps/ios/fastlane/SETUP.md:# fastlane setup (OpenHand iOS)
./apps/ios/fastlane/SETUP.md:Tip: run `scripts/ios-team-id.sh` from the repo root to print a Team ID to paste into `.env`. The helper prefers the canonical OpenHand team (`Y5PE65HELJ`) when present locally; otherwise it prefers the first non-personal team from your Xcode account (then personal team if needed). Fastlane uses this helper automatically if `IOS_DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` is missing.

./SECURITY.md:For full reporting instructions see our [Trust page](https://trust.openhand.ai).
./SECURITY.md:- Tested version details (OpenHand version and/or commit SHA).
./SECURITY.md:- Demonstrated impact tied to OpenHand's documented trust boundaries.
./SECURITY.md:- For exposed-secret reports: proof the credential is OpenHand-owned (or grants access to OpenHand-operated infrastructure/services).
./SECURITY.md:**Jamieson O'Reilly** ([@theonejvo](https://twitter.com/theonejvo)) is Security & Trust at OpenHand. Jamieson is the founder of [Dvuln](https://dvuln.com) and brings extensive experience in offensive security, penetration testing, and security program development.
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand is a labor of love. There is no bug bounty program and no budget for paid reports. Please still disclose responsibly so we can fix issues quickly.
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand does **not** model one gateway as a multi-tenant, adversarial user boundary.
./SECURITY.md:- OpenHand can technically run multiple gateway instances on one machine, but recommended operations are clean separation by trust boundary.
./SECURITY.md:- If multiple users need OpenHand, use one VPS (or host/OS user boundary) per user.
./SECURITY.md:- This is expected in OpenHand's one-user trusted-operator model. If you need isolation, enable sandbox mode (`non-main`/`all`) and keep strict tool policy.
./SECURITY.md:Plugins/extensions are part of OpenHand's trusted computing base for a gateway.
./SECURITY.md:- Using OpenHand in ways that the docs recommend not to
./SECURITY.md:- Reports that require write access to trusted local state (`~/.openhand`, workspace files like `MEMORY.md` / `memory/*.md`)
./SECURITY.md:- Exposed secrets that are third-party/user-controlled credentials (not OpenHand-owned and not granting access to OpenHand-operated infrastructure/services) without demonstrated OpenHand impact
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand security guidance assumes:
./SECURITY.md:- The host where OpenHand runs is within a trusted OS/admin boundary.
./SECURITY.md:- Anyone who can modify `~/.openhand` state/config (including `openhand.json`) is effectively a trusted operator.
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand's security model is "personal assistant" (one trusted operator, potentially many agents), not "shared multi-tenant bus."
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand separates routing from execution, but both remain inside the same operator trust boundary:
./SECURITY.md:- Plugins can execute with the same OS privileges as the OpenHand process.
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand uses a dedicated temp root for local media handoff and sandbox-adjacent temp artifacts:
./SECURITY.md:- Preferred temp root: `/tmp/openhand` (when available and safe on the host).
./SECURITY.md:- Fallback temp root: `os.tmpdir()/openhand` (or `openhand-<uid>` on multi-user hosts).
./SECURITY.md:- Sandbox media validation allows absolute temp paths only under the OpenHand-managed temp root.
./SECURITY.md:- Plugin/extension code should use OpenHand temp helpers (`resolvePreferredOpenHandTmpDir`, `buildRandomTempFilePath`, `withTempDownloadPath`) rather than raw `os.tmpdir()` defaults when handling media files.
./SECURITY.md:  - temp root resolver: `src/infra/tmp-openhand-dir.ts`
./SECURITY.md:For threat model + hardening guidance (including `openhand security audit --deep` and `--fix`), see:
./SECURITY.md:- `https://docs.openhand.ai/gateway/security`
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand's web interface (Gateway Control UI + HTTP endpoints) is intended for **local use only**.
./SECURITY.md:  - CLI: `openhand gateway run --bind loopback`.
./SECURITY.md:  - OpenHand keeps deployment flexibility by design and does not hard-forbid non-local setups.
./SECURITY.md:  - Non-local and other risky configurations are surfaced by `openhand security audit` as dangerous findings.
./SECURITY.md:  - Expected routes: `/__openhand__/canvas/`, `/__openhand__/a2ui/`.
./SECURITY.md:- The Gateway HTTP surface includes the canvas host (`/__openhand__/canvas/`, `/__openhand__/a2ui/`). Treat canvas content as sensitive/untrusted and avoid exposing it beyond loopback unless you understand the risk.
./SECURITY.md:OpenHand requires **Node.js 22.12.0 or later** (LTS). This version includes important security patches:
./SECURITY.md:When running OpenHand in Docker:
./SECURITY.md:  -v openhand-data:/app/data \
./SECURITY.md:  openhand/openhand:latest
